hero.title: Helia — Estadio, prohibition
hero.subtitle: Allende’s Chile and Helia at the civil registry; the 1973 coup; the Estadio in two documentary frames; memory sites; the 1985 prohibition list — ending with Helia.

pages.alt: Salvador Allende, President of Chile, at the United Nations, New York, c. 1970–1973 — Bernard Gotfryd, Library of Congress
11 September 1973 — bombing of La Moneda presidential palace, Santiago
1973 — detainees beside military and buses, hands on heads
Estadio Nacional — soldiers on the track, detainees massed in the lower stands, September 1973
Estadio Nacional — detainees behind chain-link at the field edge, soldiers with rifles, September 1973
Preserved detention bleachers and Un pueblo sin memoria es un pueblo sin futuro; spectators in modern seats above
Indoor memorial — suspended translucent panels with portraits
Outdoor plaques — El olvido está lleno de memoria — names of victims
Night vigil — Niños asesinados wall and candles
Sexto listado — Cerpa Pérez, Helia del Carmen
Helia Cerpa-Pérez smiling outdoors beside family, flowering tree in blossom
